task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz application is open, and the scene 'dwanko_run.tnz' is loaded, which can be verified by looking at the application title bar, the Xsheet/Timeline, or the scene browser.

task_completion.description: Scale the animation to approximately 50% size.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenToonz viewer, the animation content is visibly scaled down to take up roughly half the width and height of the camera frame boundary. The Animate tool or scale parameters might be visible.

task_completion.description: Position the scaled animation in the bottom-right corner.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenToonz viewer, the scaled animation is located entirely within the bottom-right quadrant of the camera frame. The top, left, and bottom-left areas of the camera frame should appear empty.

task_completion.description: Configure the render output settings to the correct directory and format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Output Settings' dialog in OpenToonz is open, showing the 'Save In' path set to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/pip_corner/' (or a close variation) and the file format set to 'png'.

task_completion.description: Render the result as a PNG sequence of at least 24 frames.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rendering progress bar is visible in OpenToonz, or a file manager/terminal window shows a sequence of multiple PNG files (at least 24) successfully generated in the '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/pip_corner/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenToonz GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the OpenToonz interface (menus, tools, viewer, output settings) rather than terminal commands using external tools like FFmpeg or ImageMagick to manipulate the images.

integrity.description: The scaling and positioning were done within the OpenToonz scene.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz viewer should show the camera frame with the animation scaled and positioned correctly before rendering, rather than the agent modifying the rendered images post-hoc.

integrity.description: The agent did not create dummy files to bypass the frame count check.
integrity.visual_evidence: The output directory should contain actual rendered image files with thumbnails or file sizes indicating real image data, not empty files created via terminal commands like 'touch'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The source scene must be '/home/ga/OpenToonz/samples/dwanko_run.tnz'. The output must be rendered to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/pip_corner' as a PNG sequence containing at least 24 frames. The animation must be positioned in the bottom-right quadrant; programmatically, this is verified by ensuring the pixel density in the bottom-right is at least 3.0 times higher than in the top-left quadrant.
